title: lavatera triloba subsp. minoricensis
intro: Lavatera triloba subsp. minoricensis is a subspecies of the annual or biennial flowering plant known as the 'shrubmallow' or 'tree mallow', characterized by its pink to white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lavatera triloba subsp. minoricensis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in skincare products due to its antibacterial and moisturizing properties.
